_schema_note: One record per bot. null = unknown, run scraper.py to fill. Fields marked _prior are hand-set estimates to be replaced by scraped/trained values.

bots.name: Tombstone
bots.weapon_detail: Interchangeable horizontal bar, ~65-84 lb blades, 2-wheel invertible drive

self_failure_incidents.event: World Championship I final vs Bite Force
self_failure_incidents.failure: Weapon died from overheated batteries; lost judges' decision
Stuck on its own weapon chain

self_failure_incidents.event: World Championship V final vs Bite Force
self_failure_incidents.failure: Weapon stopped (bad battery); reduced to pushing match in second half

bots.defect_summary: Chronic recoil-induced self-damage. Weapon/battery systems fail late in fights. Documented tendency to disable itself with the recoil of its own weapon.
bots.exploit: Survive the opening minute. Absorb or deflect early hits; win probability shifts sharply once the fight goes long and his weapon or batteries fail.

bots.name: End Game
bots.weapon_detail: Single-toothed vertical spinner, 40-55 lb disk options, piano-key wedgelets (2022)

self_failure_incidents.failure: Flipped; could not self-right because disk was not up to speed; counted out
Radio receiver destroyed; counted out
Lost drive on one side; struggled to show movement; counted out
Stopped moving under pulverizer pressure; counted out
Rendered immobile; counted out

bots.defect_summary: Loses by count-out, not destruction. Immobilization-prone: one-sided drive failures, exposed electronics (destroyed radio receiver), and early builds could not self-right unless the disk was spinning. A secondary self-righting mechanism was added in later seasons to patch this.
bots.exploit: Kill the mobility, not the bot. Target drive pods and force it inverted while its weapon is down; historically it gets counted out rather than knocked out.
bots.notable: Only bot to defeat three World Champions (Tombstone, Tantrum, SawBlaze). Beat Tombstone and Whiplash by out-of-the-arena KO.
bots.data_completeness: high — record and incidents scraped via Bright Data; ko_win_rate still needs fight-table parse (scraper.py TODO)

bots.name: Bite Force
bots.weapon_detail: Hydraulic jaws / grappler hybrid with a pneumatic flipping arm
bots.defect_summary: No recurring failure pattern documented across two championship runs — one of the most reliable bots in the dataset. Tracks are a known structural target (opponents have tried to rip them off) but that has not yet produced a documented loss.
bots.exploit: No exploitable pattern found. If anything, target the tracks/undercarriage — the one structural vulnerability opponents have gone after — but treat this as unproven, not a plan.
bots.data_completeness: partial — record scraped via Bright Data; single career loss not yet located in text, no fight-by-fight breakdown parsed

bots.name: Witch Doctor
bots.weapon_detail: Asymmetrical vertical spinning disk, twin hinged forks

self_failure_incidents.event: World Championship VI final vs Tantrum
self_failure_incidents.failure: Lost by KO in the championship final despite a 2-1 Fight Night record
Documented trivia: both times Witch Doctor faced Tombstone, the winner ended up with a broken weapon — weapon reliability decides the fight either way
Runner-up in two separate World Championships (IV and VI) without ever winning the title — a repeated 'best of the rest' pattern

bots.defect_summary: Extremely durable and tournament-consistent (only bot to qualify for all seven World Championships) but has a documented ceiling: two runner-up finishes without a title, and its highest-profile losses come in finals rather than early exits.
bots.exploit: Force the fight to a final. Witch Doctor's record shows it wins rounds reliably but has lost every championship match it has reached — pressure in the final round is where it has folded historically.
bots.data_completeness: high — 41-18 competitive record and title history scraped via Bright Data; fight-by-fight KO/JD breakdown not yet parsed

bots.name: Minotaur
bots.weapon_detail: 60-70lb drum spinner, self-rights using the drum's own gyroscopic force

self_failure_incidents.failure: Became high-centered on floor damage after its own recoil sheared chassis bolts; weapon stopped and it was counted out
Weapon underperformed after support-pole damage; lost on judges' decision
Lost a drive belt mid-fight, weapon stopped, then became high-centered and was counted out
Battery ruptured and caught fire after a direct hit; lost by judges' decision

bots.defect_summary: Self-righting is entirely dependent on its own spinning drum for gyroscopic force. Once the weapon fails or stops (belt slip, battery puncture, sheared bolts from its own recoil), Minotaur cannot right itself and becomes an easy count-out target — despite frequently being the more aggressive bot for most of the fight.
bots.exploit: Survive the weapon exchanges without an early loss, and specifically target the drum drivetrain (belts, battery mounts). Once the weapon stops, Minotaur has no backup way to self-right.
bots.data_completeness: partial — rich incident history scraped via Bright Data; exact win/loss totals not yet parsed
